transcript: 藞br瑟s蓹l w瑟冒 re瑟d蕭
definitions: To become visibly and intensely angry, often to the point of showing physical signs of agitation.
To be filled with extreme anger or indignation.
examples: He would bristle with rage at the slightest insult.
The manager began to bristle with rage when he heard the news.
She bristled with rage as the accusations flew.
